mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in 70%+ of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns drops below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening economic demand, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >50% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline, or the joint effect size falls below 0.05 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of periods with UNRATE > 5%, or the coefficient on UNRATE in predictive models is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with INDPRO growth, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising housing permits (HOUST) signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST rise, or the interaction term in regression models is not significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) in a low-unemployment environment signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 1% in 60%+ of periods with PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 4%, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A dovish shift in monetary policy (FEDFUNDS decline) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) creates a 'stagflation' risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL > 3%, or the interaction term in predictive models is not significant (p > 0.1).
